title: agropyron repens subvar. pycnanthum
intro: Agropyron repens subvar. pycnanthum, also known as crested wheatgrass, is a type of grass native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of crested wheatgrass include its ability to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote digestive health. In herbal preparations, crested wheatgrass can be consumed as a tea, infused oil, or dried powder, and is often used to support overall well-being and reduce stress.
